sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Celta Vigo, sitting at 7th place with 46 points, host Villarreal, who are in 5th place with 58 points, in Round 33 of the league at the Balaídos Stadium in Vigo. This matchup promises to be an exciting clash as both teams are in the upper half of the table, with Villarreal holding a significant 12-point lead over their opponents. The referee, Javier Alberola Rojas, oversees the game, known for averaging 3.6 yellow cards and 0.17 red cards per match, which could influence the physicality of the encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Villarreal have dominated with 3 wins compared to Celta Vigo's 1 win and 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on August 26, 2024, saw Villarreal edge out Celta Vigo in a thrilling 4-3 victory at home, with Sergi Cardona earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.8 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show that matches between these teams often produce goals, with 4 out of the last 5 games seeing over 2.5 goals, and both teams scoring in 5 out of 5 recent encounters.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Villarreal come into this game with a streak of 4 matches without a loss, showcasing their resilience on the road. They also have a trend of high-scoring games, with 4 out of their last 5 matches featuring over 2.5 goals. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, have struggled defensively at home, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 matches and conceding first in 5 of their last 7 games. However, both teams scoring in 5 of Celta Vigo’s last 7 home games suggests they can pose an attacking threat.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Villarreal, Ayoze Pérez stands out as a major threat, ranked 4th in the league for goals scored with 16 goals, significantly overperforming his expected goals (xG) of 11.16 by 4.8391. He’s in fine form too, with 3 goals and 2 assists in his last 5 matches, boasting a 7.48 average Sofascore rating. Thierno Barry is another player to watch for Villarreal, contributing 11 goals this season. On Celta Vigo’s side, Iago Aspas leads the attack with 9 goals and 4 assists, supported by Borja Iglesias, who also has 9 goals and is in form with 3 goals in his last 5 matches at a 7.125 Sofascore rating.

sections.subtitle: Notable Absences
sections.text: Villarreal will feel the absence of key midfielder Alejandro Baena, ranked 3rd in the league for assists with 8 and 1st for key passes with 88, alongside defender Sergi Cardona, who has 7 assists this season. Their absence could impact Villarreal’s creativity and defensive stability. Celta Vigo, meanwhile, will miss defender Carl Starfelt, which may further weaken their backline, already struggling with conceding in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Style of Play
sections.text: Celta Vigo rank 5th in the league for total goals scored with 52, matching Villarreal’s output, and are also 5th for big chances created with 82. They excel in possession, averaging 53.74% (5th in the league), and accurate passing at 85.34% (4th in the league), suggesting a controlled style of play. However, their defense is a concern, conceding 52 goals (17th in least goals conceded) and ranking poorly in ground (18th) and aerial duels won (17th) at 46.8% and 47.0%, respectively. Villarreal, on the other hand, show a balanced goal distribution across game periods, with 19.1% of their goals coming between the 16th and 30th minutes, indicating an ability to strike early.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: Under referee Javier Alberola Rojas, who averages 3.6 yellow cards per game, discipline will be crucial. Celta Vigo have accumulated 74 yellow cards this season (9th fewest in the league) at an average of 2.18 per game, while recent trends show 4 out of their last 5 home games have had fewer than 4.5 cards. In head-to-head matchups, 4 out of the last 5 games between these teams have seen over 4.5 cards, hinting at a potentially feisty affair. Set-piece-wise, Celta Vigo average only 3.82 corners per game (18th in the league), and 8 out of their last 9 home games have had fewer than 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context and Standout Performances
sections.text: Marcos Alonso of Celta Vigo has been a consistent performer against Villarreal in the last 5 matchups, starting 4 times with an impressive average Sofascore rating of 7.775, though without goals or assists. His defensive stability could be key for Celta Vigo in this fixture. Villarreal, meanwhile, have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 5 away games against Celta Vigo, a trend that could give hope to the home side’s attacking unit, including players like Óscar Mingueza, who has contributed 6 assists this season.
